devanagari: वसुदेवमुखेन भगवत्तत्त्वप्रतिपादनं भगवता देवकीप्रार्थनया तदीय मृतपुत्राणां आनयनं च । श्रीबादरायणिरुवाच । अथैकदात्मजौ प्राप्तौ कृतपादाभिव...
english_devnagari: vasudevamukhena bhagavattattvapratipādanaṃ bhagavatā devakīprārthanayā tadīya mṛtaputrāṇāṃ ānayanaṃ ca śrībādarāyaṇiruvāca athaikadātmajau prāptau kṛtapādābhivandanau vasudevo

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[vocative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[imperative active second plural], [injunctive middle third single]

translation:
The Son of Bādarāyaṇa (Śrī Śuka) said:
On one occasion, (after their return from the sacrifice at Kurukṣetra), Balarāma and Śrī Kṛṣṇa went (as usual) (to pay respects to their parents), and bowed down to Vasudeva. Vasudeva affectionately received them when they saluted his feet, and spoke to his sons as follows:—

devanagari: मुनीनां स वचः श्रुत्वा पुत्रयोर्धामसूचकम् । तद्वीर्यैर्जातविश्रम्भः परिभाष्याभ्यभाषत ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: munīnāṃ sa vacaḥ śrutvā putrayordhāmasūcakam tadvīryairjātaviśrambhaḥ paribhāṣyābhyabhāṣata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 4 verb]
[vocative single from √ jan class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 4 verb]

translation: Having heard the speech of the sages indicating the glorious powers of their sons and feeling a sense of confidence by their exploits, he affectionately addressed them and said.

devanagari: कृष्ण कृष्ण महायोगिन्सङ्कर्षण सनातन । जाने वामस्य यत्साक्षात्प्रधानपुरुषौ परौ ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇa kṛṣṇa mahāyoginsaṅkarṣaṇa sanātana jāne vāmasya yatsākṣātpradhānapuruṣau parau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: O Kṛṣṇa! Kṛṣṇa! O great Yogin! O eternal Saṅkarṣaṇa! I (now) realize that both of you are the Supreme Persons (ruling over) Prakṛti and Puruṣa, the direct causes of the universe.

devanagari: यत्र येन यतो यस्य यस्मै यद्यद्यथा यदा । स्यादिदं भगवान्साक्षात्प्रधानपुरुषेश्वरः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: yatra yena yato yasya yasmai yadyadyathā yadā syādidaṃ bhagavānsākṣātpradhānapuruṣeśvaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: You are the place (Substratum), the agent and the instrument of the universe. You are its source and its object or purpose; whenever or whatever form it assumes is yourself. As and when this universe evolves, all the causes thereof including time and manner are the Almighty Lord yourself who control both the Prakṛti (to be enjoyed) and Puruṣa (the enjoyer) and transcend them both.

devanagari: एतन्नानाविधं विश्वमात्मसृष्टमधोक्षज । आत्मनानुप्रविश्यात्मन्प्राणो जीवो बिभर्ष्यज ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: etannānāvidhaṃ viśvamātmasṛṣṭamadhokṣaja ātmanānupraviśyātmanprāṇo jīvo bibharṣyaja

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: O Supra-sensuous Lord! Having created this universe of a diversified nature out of yourself, you enter it as its soul, the Indwelling Controller, and you, the eternal (unborn) Lord, become the active force (Prāṇa) and the cognitive force (Jīva) and maintain it.

devanagari: प्राणादीनां विश्वसृजां शक्तयो याः परस्य ताः । पारतन्त्र्याद्वै सादृश्याद् द्वयोश्चेष्टैव चेष्टताम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: prāṇādīnāṃ viśvasṛjāṃ śaktayo yāḥ parasya tāḥ pāratantryādvai sādṛśyād dvayośceṣṭaiva ceṣṭatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]

translation: [1] The powers with which Prāṇa and Sūtra are endowed and other forces which go to create the universe, are really the potencies of the Supreme Self, for these powers depend (for their existence on you and there is a great dissimilarity between, (the nature of) Prāṇa and the Supreme Soul, (the former is physical or material while the latter is spiritual). The activity of Prāṇa and others is only apparent (the real motive force is you, just as the velocity of an arrow is not in the arrow itself but in the physical force of the man who discharged it.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
The verses following these are the echoes of Vibhūti Yoga. Bhagavad Gītā. Ch.X.

devanagari: कान्तिस्तेजः प्रभा सत्ता चन्द्राग्न्यर्कर्क्षविद्युताम् । यत्स्थैर्यं भूभृतां भूमेर्वृत्तिर्गन्धोऽर्थतो भवान् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: kāntistejaḥ prabhā sattā candrāgnyarkarkṣavidyutām yatsthairyaṃ bhūbhṛtāṃ bhūmervṛttirgandho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The lovely light of the moon, the heat and glare of the fire, the brilliant splendour of the sun, the twinkling of stars and the streaks of lightning, the stability of mountains, fragrance which characterises the earth—all these special characteristics are as a matter of fact yourself only.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
na tatra sūryo bhāti, na candra-tārakam, etc. tameva bhāntam anubhāti sarvaṃ, tasya bhāsā sarvam idaṃ vibhāti /—Kaṭha Up. 5.15 Śveta. Up. 8.14. Muṇḍaka 2.2.10 Śvetāśvatara.
Also yadādityagataṃ tejo, etc. in Bhagavad Gītā. 15.12.

devanagari: तर्पणं प्राणनमपां देव त्वं ताश्च तद्रसः । ओजः सहो बलं चेष्टा गतिर्वायोस्तवेश्वर ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tarpaṇaṃ prāṇanamapāṃ deva tvaṃ tāśca tadrasaḥ ojaḥ saho balaṃ ceṣṭā gatirvāyostaveśvara

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active first single], [aorist active first single]

translation: The qualities of water, viz. satisfying the thirst, the life-giving effect, are yourself, O God. The waters themselves and their essential characteristics, viz. liquidity and taste are yourself. The energy of senses, the mental power (firm willpower), bodily strength, the activity of the body, the activities and the force of the wind are your qualities (though they are attributed to the wind element).

devanagari: दिशां त्वमवकाशोऽसि दिशः खं स्फोट आश्रयः । नादो वर्णस्त्वमॐकार आकृतीनां पृथक्कृतिः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: diśāṃ tvamavakāśo nādo varṇastvamaoṃkāra ākṛtīnāṃ pṛthakkṛtiḥ
translation: You are the space that is delimited by cardinal points; You are the quarters themselves and the ether with its characteristic the Subtle sound (sphoṭa) which is the parā form of speech. You are the sound Om, the Paśyantī form of speech. You are (the Madhyamā form of speech) and the articulate speech) (Vaikharī) which separates letter sounds and words which denote different objects.

devanagari: इन्द्रियं त्विन्द्रियाणां त्वं देवाश्च तदनुग्रहः । अवबोधो भवान्बुद्धेर्जीवस्यानुस्मृतिः सती ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: indriyaṃ tvindriyāṇāṃ tvaṃ devāśca tadanugrahaḥ avabodho bhavānbuddherjīvasyānusmṛtiḥ satī
translation: You are the real sense organ, the power revealing its special objects; You are also the presiding deities of the senses and their potency. You are the cognitive and retentive faculty of the Intellect. You are the co-ordinating power of different experiences inherent in Jīva.

devanagari: भूतानामसि भूतादिरिन्द्रियाणां च तैजसः । वैकारिको विकल्पानां प्रधानमनुशायिनम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūtānāmasi bhūtādirindriyāṇāṃ ca taijasaḥ vaikāriko vikalpānāṃ pradhānamanuśāyinam
translation: You are the Tamasa aspect of Ego (Ahaṃkāra) which is the root cause of the gross elements; the Rājasa type (Tayasa) of Ego, as the cause of the sense-organs; the Sāttvika kind of Ego (Vaikārika) which creates the deities presiding over the senses and the mind (and the Pradhāna or Māyā which makes jīvas wander in the Saṃsāra[3] (or: You are the Chief Prakṛti, the cause of Mahat and other principles and which involves the jīvas in Saṃsāra.)
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: You constitute the Karma which makes jīva experience the fruit of the balance of his Karmas.

devanagari: नश्वरेष्विह भावेषु तदसि त्वमनश्वरम् । यथा द्रव्यविकारेषु द्रव्यमात्रं निरूपितम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: naśvareṣviha bhāveṣu tadasi tvamanaśvaram yathā dravyavikāreṣu dravyamātraṃ nirūpitam
translation: Just as the material substance (e.g. gold) is the constant factor all through its various modifications (e.g. various ornaments like bangles, armlets, etc.), you are the constant factor abiding in all these perishable objects and are not subject to modification or decay

devanagari: सत्त्वम्रजस्तम इति गुणास्तद्वृत्तयश्च याः । त्वय्यद्धा ब्रह्मणि परे कल्पिता योगमायया ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sattvamrajastama iti guṇāstadvṛttayaśca yāḥ tvayyaddhā brahmaṇi pare kalpitā yogamāyayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ kḷp ], [vocative plural from √ kḷp ]
[nominative plural from √ kḷp ], [vocative plural from √ kḷp ], [accusative plural from √ kḷp ]

translation: The attributes of Prakṛti viz. Sattva, Rajas and Tamas and their products (such as Mahat and other principles) have been superimposed on you, the veritable Brahman, by your Yoga Māyā (but you are untouched by them)[4].
purport:
Footnote [4]:
mayā tatam idaṃ sarvaṃ jagad avyakta-mūrtinā /
matsthāni sarva-bhutāni na cāham teṣvavasthitaḥ //
  —Bhagavad Gītā 9.4

devanagari: तस्मान्न सन्त्यमी भावा यर्हि त्वयि विकल्पिताः । त्वं चामीषु विकारेषु ह्यन्यदा व्यावहारिकः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmānna santyamī bhāvā yarhi tvayi vikalpitāḥ tvaṃ cāmīṣu vikāreṣu hyanyadā vyāvahārikaḥ
translation: These created things, therefore, do not really exist, when they are not seen as projected on you (at the time of Pralaya) by Yoga Māyā; they are non-existent, (i.e. their very existence depends on you as the substratum on which they are superimposed). You abide in them (when you create and sustain the universe). Otherwise you remain unconnected with these modifications and abide in your absolute (blissful) state.

devanagari: गुणप्रवाह एतस्मिन्नबुधास्त्वखिलात्मनः । गतिं सूक्ष्मामबोधेन संसरन्तीह कर्मभिः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: guṇapravāha etasminnabudhāstvakhilātmanaḥ gatiṃ sūkṣmāmabodhena saṃsarantīha karmabhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural]

[nominative dual from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ sṛ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ sṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Those who are ignorant of the subtle course of yours who are the soul of all, and are present in this universe or Saṃsāra, which is a constant flow of guṇas, (wrongly identify themselves with their bodies through their ignorance and there by) get enmeshed by their Karmas, and continue to whirl in the Saṃsāra, as a consequence of those Karmas.

devanagari: यदृच्छया नृतां प्राप्य सुकल्पामिह दुर्लभाम् । स्वार्थे प्रमत्तस्य वयो गतं त्वन्माययेश्वर ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: yadṛcchayā nṛtāṃ prāpya sukalpāmiha durlabhām svārthe pramattasya vayo gataṃ tvanmāyayeśvara
translation: (I sincerely regret that) after having attained by a lucky chance, birth here (in Bhārata Varṣa) as a man endowed with vigorous sense-organs—which is really very difficult to obtain, I remained negligent about the main object of human life (viz. Pursuit of Mokṣa) through the influence of your Māyā and have wasted my life, O Lord.

devanagari: असावहम्ममैवैते देहे चास्यान्वयादिषु । स्नेहपाशैर्निबध्नाति भवान्सर्वमिदं जगत् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: asāvahammamaivaite dehe cāsyānvayādiṣu snehapāśairnibadhnāti bhavānsarvamidaṃ jagat
translation: Your Lordship binds down the whole of the world with bonds of affection to the body as being my own self and relations and belongings of the body (such as sons, etc.) as they are absolutely mine.

devanagari: युवां न नः सुतौ साक्षात्प्रधानपुरुषेश्वरौ । भूभारक्षत्रक्षपण अवतीर्णौ तथात्थ ह ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: yuvāṃ na naḥ sutau sākṣātpradhānapuruṣeśvarau bhūbhārakṣatrakṣapaṇa avatīrṇau tathāttha ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ su class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ su class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ su class 5 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sū class 6 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 6 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 6 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: I realize that both of you are not our sons but directly the controllers of Prakṛti and Puruṣa (The Primordial Matter and individual souls). As you have stated (at the time of your incarnation in our prison-cell), both of you have taken this descent (in human form) for the annihilation of the Kṣattriya Kings who have become a burden to the earth.

devanagari: तत्ते गतोऽस्म्यरणमद्य पदारविन्दम् । आपन्नसंसृतिभयापहमार्तबन्धो । एतावतालमलमिन्द्रियलालसेन । मर्त्यात्मदृक्त्वयि परे यदपत्यबुद्धिः ॥ १९...
english_devnagari: tatte gato āpannasaṃsṛtibhayāpahamārtabandho etāvatālamalamindriyalālasena martyātmadṛktvayi pare yadapatyabuddhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: O friend of the afflicted! I have, therefore, now sought asylum with your lotus-feet which dispel the fear of Saṃsāra of those who resort to them (your feet). Absolutely enough of this craving for objects of sense-enjoyment under the influence of which I looked upon this mortal body as my soul and you who are the supreme person, as my child.

devanagari: सूतीगृहे ननु जगाद भवानजो नौ । सञ्जज्ञ इत्यनुयुगं निजधर्मगुप्त्यै । नानातनूर्गगनवद्विदधज्जहासि । को वेद भूम्न उरुगाय विभूतिमायाम् ॥ २० ...
english_devnagari: sūtīgṛhe nanu jagāda bhavānajo nau sañjajña ityanuyugaṃ nijadharmaguptyai nānātanūrgaganavadvidadhajjahāsi ko veda bhūmna urugāya vibhūtimāyām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Indeed, you did tell both of us in the lying-inchamber, that you are. as a matter of fact unborn (not actually born) but for the protection of the order of righteousness (ordained by you), you reveal yourself as “born” from us (formerly as Sutapas and Pṛśni, Kāśyapa and Diti, now Vasudeva and Devakī) in every Yuga. You assume various forms like the sky and discard them (though you are one), O Lord whose glory is widely sung(in Śrutis). Who can comprehend the wonderful Māyā (Potency) of yours, O Omnipresent Lord?

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । आकर्ण्येत्थं पितुर्वाक्यं भगवान्सात्वतर्षभः । प्रत्याह प्रश्रयानम्रः प्रहसन्श्लक्ष्णया गिरा ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ākarṇyetthaṃ piturvākyaṃ bhagavānsātvatarṣabhaḥ pratyāha praśrayānamraḥ prahasanślakṣṇayā girā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[nominative single from √ vak class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vak class 1 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Hearing such a speech from his father, the glorious Lord, the leader of Sātvatas (Śrī Kṛṣṇa) smiled. Saluting his father with all humility, he replied in sweet words.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । वचो वः समवेतार्थं तातैतदुपमन्महे । यन्नः पुत्रान्समुद्दिश्य तत्त्वग्राम उदाहृतः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca vaco vaḥ samavetārthaṃ tātaitadupamanmahe yannaḥ putrānsamuddiśya tattvagrāma udāhṛtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation:
The Lord said:
Your speech is pregnant with philosophical significance. O father, and we accept it; as thereby you have expounded to us, your sons, the whole body of Philosophic principles.

devanagari: अहं यूयमसावार्य इमे च द्वारकौकसः । सर्वेऽप्येवं यदुश्रेष्ठ विमृग्याः सचराचरम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ yūyamasāvārya ime ca dvārakaukasaḥ sarve

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 9 verb]

[nominative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 6 verb], [nominative single from √ yu class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ yu class 9 verb]
[present middle first single], [present passive first single], [imperfect middle first single], [imperfect passive first single]

translation: I, you, this elder brother Balarāma, all these inhabitants of Dvārakā and all the mobile and immobile creation should be regarded as such (Brahman), O the greatest of Yadus.

devanagari: आत्मा ह्येकः स्वयंज्योतिर्नित्योऽन्यो निर्गुणो गुणैः । आत्मसृष्टैस्तत्कृतेषु भूतेषु बहुधेयते ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmā hyekaḥ svayaṃjyotirnityo ātmasṛṣṭaistatkṛteṣu bhūteṣu bahudheyate
translation: The (Supreme) Soul is really one, self-illuminating, eternal, different and distinct from the body, devoid of any attributes; it is however due to his alliance with guṇas created by him and in bodies of various creatures produced out of those guṇas, that he appears diversified (as a man, a god, etc.), visible, momentary, identical with the body and possessed of attributes.

devanagari: खं वायुर्ज्योतिरापो भूस्तत्कृतेषु यथाशयम् । आविस्तिरोऽल्पभूर्येको नानात्वं यात्यसावपि ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: khaṃ vāyurjyotirāpo bhūstatkṛteṣu yathāśayam āvistiro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ yat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yat ]
[vocative single from √ yat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ yat ]

translation: Just as the elements, sky, air, fire, water and earth become diversified (as small and great, manifest, and unmanifest, etc.) according to their products, so does the soul, though one, assume different forms and becomes many, manifest or unmanifest, small or great.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । एवं भगवता राजन्वसुदेव उदाहृतः । श्रुत्वा विनष्टनानाधीस्तूष्णीं प्रीतमना अभूत् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca evaṃ bhagavatā rājanvasudeva udāhṛtaḥ śrutvā vinaṣṭanānādhīstūṣṇīṃ prītamanā abhūt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
In this way, O king Parīkṣit, Vasudeva who was thus enlightened by Kṛṣṇa became divested of the notion of diversity, remained silent and delighted at heart.

devanagari: अथ तत्र कुरुश्रेष्ठ देवकी सर्वदेवता । श्रुत्वानीतं गुरोः पुत्रमात्मजाभ्यां सुविस्मिता ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: atha tatra kuruśreṣṭha devakī sarvadevatā śrutvānītaṃ guroḥ putramātmajābhyāṃ suvismitā
translation: Devakī, the embodiment of all deities was there at this time (of conversation). She was wonderstruck, O prominent-most Kuru, to hear that her sons had brought back to life, the son of their preceptor.

devanagari: कृष्णरामौ समाश्राव्य पुत्रान्कंसविहिंसितान् । स्मरन्ती कृपणं प्राह वैक्लव्यादश्रुलोचना ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇarāmau samāśrāvya putrānkaṃsavihiṃsitān smarantī kṛpaṇaṃ prāha vaiklavyādaśrulocanā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: Addressing Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Balarāma, Devakī, who remembered her sons killed by Kaṃsa, piteously appealed to them, with tears of grief flowing down from her eyes.

devanagari: श्रीदेवक्युवाच । राम रामाप्रमेयात्मन्कृष्ण योगेश्वरेश्वर । वेदाहं वां विश्वसृजामीश्वरावादिपूरुषौ ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīdevakyuvāca rāma rāmāprameyātmankṛṣṇa yogeśvareśvara vedāhaṃ vāṃ viśvasṛjāmīśvarāvādipūruṣau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Devakī said:
O Balarāma, Delighter of hearts and of immeasurable prowess! O Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of the masters of Yoga! I recognize you both as the Lords of the progenitors of the universe and the First persons in the universe.

devanagari: कलविध्वस्तसत्त्वानां राज्ञामुच्छास्त्रवर्तिनाम् । भूमेर्भारायमाणानामवतीर्णौ किलाद्य मे ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: kalavidhvastasattvānāṃ rājñāmucchāstravartinām bhūmerbhārāyamāṇānāmavatīrṇau kilādya me
translation: It is (traditionally) reported that both of you have been born of me for the extermination of Kings whose virtue (or Sāttvika nature) is lost through the influence of the Timespirit and who transgress the injunctions (you laid down for their behaviour) in the scriptures (Śāstras) and have proved to be a burden to the earth.

devanagari: यस्यांशांशांशभागेन विश्वोत्पत्तिलयोदयाः । भवन्ति किल विश्वात्मंस्तं त्वाद्याहं गतिं गता ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: yasyāṃśāṃśāṃśabhāgena viśvotpattilayodayāḥ bhavanti kila viśvātmaṃstaṃ tvādyāhaṃ gatiṃ gatā
translation: O Soul of the Universe (Śrī Kṛṣṇa)! I resort to you for shelter to-day—you who bring about the creation, sustenance and dissolution of the Universe by your minutest part, viz. the particles of the guṇas (Sattva, Rajas and Tamas) which are evolved out of a part of Māyā which in its own way is an evolute of a part of Puruṣa who is but a part manifestation of you.

devanagari: चिरान्मृतसुतादाने गुरुणा किल चोदितौ । आनिन्यथुः पितृस्थानाद्गुरवे गुरुदक्षिणाम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: cirānmṛtasutādāne guruṇā kila coditau āninyathuḥ pitṛsthānādgurave gurudakṣiṇām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [vocative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]
[vocative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]
[nominative dual from √ cud ], [vocative dual from √ cud ], [accusative dual from √ cud ]

translation: Both of you, being urged by Time and desired by your preceptor Sānḍīpani to bring back to life his son who died long ago, you brought back his son from the region of Pitṛs (manes) i.e. the abode of death, and presented him to your preceptor as a homage (and thus paid the debt of your preceptorship)

devanagari: तथा मे कुरुतं कामं युवां योगेश्वरेश्वरौ । भोजराजहतान्पुत्रान्कामये द्रष्टुमाहृतान् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tathā me kurutaṃ kāmaṃ yuvāṃ yogeśvareśvarau bhojarājahatānputrānkāmaye draṣṭumāhṛtān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: You should similarly fulfil my desire as both of you are the Lords of the masters of Yoga. I desire to see, brought to life, my sons who have been killed by Kaṃsa, the King of Bhojas.

devanagari: ऋषिरुवाच । एवं सञ्चोदितौ मात्रा रामः कृष्णश्च भारत । सुतलं संविविशतुर्योगमायामुपाश्रितौ ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣiruvāca evaṃ sañcoditau mātrā rāmaḥ kṛṣṇaśca bhārata sutalaṃ saṃviviśaturyogamāyāmupāśritau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
The sage Śuka said:
Directed thus by their mother, O Parīkṣit, Balarāma and Kṛṣṇa resorted to their Yogamāyā (Super-human Yogic potency) and entered the nether world called Sutala.

devanagari: तस्मिन्प्रविष्टावुपलभ्य दैत्यराड् । विश्वात्मदैवं सुतरां तथात्मनः । तद्दर्शनाह्लादपरिप्लुताशयः । सद्यः समुत्थाय ननाम सान्वयः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasminpraviṣṭāvupalabhya daityarāḍ viśvātmadaivaṃ sutarāṃ tathātmanaḥ taddarśanāhlādapariplutāśayaḥ sadyaḥ samutthāya nanāma sānvayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Beholding both of them enter Sutala, King Bali recognised them to be the Deity, the Soul of the Universe and especially his own God (Lord Viṣṇu). His heart was overflowing with joy at their appearance. Instantaneously, he rose up from his seat and along with the members of his family, he prostrated himself before them.

devanagari: तयोः समानीय वरासनं मुदा निविष्टयोस्तत्र महात्मनोस्तयोः । दधार पादाववनिज्य तज्जलं सवृन्द आब्रह्म पुनद्यदम्बु ह ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: tayoḥ samānīya varāsanaṃ mudā niviṣṭayostatra mahātmanostayoḥ dadhāra pādāvavanijya tajjalaṃ savṛnda ābrahma punadyadambu ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ pū class 9 verb], [vocative single from √ pū class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ pū class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: He brought for those great-souled personages excellent seats. When they gladly occupied them, he washed their feet and sprinkled over his head as well as those of the members of the family present, that water which (as they say traditionally) purifies the world right from its creator, god Brahmā.

devanagari: समर्हयामास स तौ विभूतिभिर्महार्हवस्त्राभरणानुलेपनैः । ताम्बूलदीपामृतभक्षणादिभिः स्वगोत्रवित्तात्मसमर्पणेन च ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: samarhayāmāsa sa tau vibhūtibhirmahārhavastrābharaṇānulepanaiḥ tāmbūladīpāmṛtabhakṣaṇādibhiḥ svagotravittātmasamarpaṇena ca
translation: He worshipped them by offering extremely valuable cloths, ornaments, fragrant ointments, betel leaves, chauries (waving around their faces) lamps, catering nectar-like sweet dishes and by submitting to their services his own self, his race and his wealth.

devanagari: स इन्द्रसेनो भगवत्पदाम्बुजं बिभ्रन्मुहुः प्रेमविभिन्नया धिया । उवाच हानन्दजलाकुलेक्षणः प्रहृष्टरोमा नृप गद्गदाक्षरम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa indraseno bhagavatpadāmbujaṃ bibhranmuhuḥ premavibhinnayā dhiyā uvāca hānandajalākulekṣaṇaḥ prahṛṣṭaromā nṛpa gadgadākṣaram

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ hā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ hā class 1 verb]

translation: It is traditionally reported that Bali whose army equalled to that of Indra, the King of gods had his heart so much soaked with love for his God that he bore the Lord’s feet on his head again and again. His eyes were bedimmed with tears of joy and his hair, standing on their ends. His voice being choked with emotions, he prayed in faltering accents.

devanagari: बलिरुवाच । नमोऽनन्ताय बृहते नमः कृष्णाय वेधसे । साङ्ख्ययोगवितानाय ब्रह्मणे परमात्मने ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: baliruvāca namo sāṅkhyayogavitānāya brahmaṇe paramātmane

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Bali Prayed:
Salutations to you the Infinite (Lord Śeṣa) who are so vast (as to bear the universe on your hood). I bow to you Lord Kṛṣṇa, the embodiment of eternal bliss, the creator of the world, the expounder of the Sāṅkhya and Yoga, darśanas (or the Paths of knowledge and Bhakti), the Supreme Soul, nay the very Brahman himself.

devanagari: दर्शनं वां हि भूतानां दुष्प्रापं चाप्यदुर्लभम् । रजस्तमः स्वभावानां यन्नः प्राप्तौ यदृच्छया ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: darśanaṃ vāṃ hi bhūtānāṃ duṣprāpaṃ cāpyadurlabham rajastamaḥ svabhāvānāṃ yannaḥ prāptau yadṛcchayā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Even your sight is very difficult to obtain to all beings (even to the masters of yoga). It is through your Grace that (to some) it is not so very difficult, (just as now) you of your own accord have luckily blessed us with your visit even though we are dominated by rajas and tamas by nature.

devanagari: दैत्यदानवगन्धर्वाः सिद्धविद्याध्रचारणाः । यक्षरक्षः पिशाचाश्च भूतप्रमथनायकाः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: daityadānavagandharvāḥ siddhavidyādhracāraṇāḥ yakṣarakṣaḥ piśācāśca bhūtapramathanāyakāḥ
translation: We, Daityas, Dānavas, Gandharvas, Siddhas, Vidyādharas, Cāraṇas Yakṣas Ogres (Rākṣasas), goblins (Piśācas), the leaders of evil spirits like Bhūtas and Pramathas and others of our ilk, bear continuous hostility to you who are the receptacle of pure Sattva (unsullied by Rajas and Tamas) and the very embodiment of scriptures (Śāstras).

devanagari: विशुद्धसत्त्वधाम्न्यद्धा त्वयि शास्त्रशरीरिणि । नित्यं निबद्धवैरास्ते वयं चान्ये च तादृशाः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: viśuddhasattvadhāmnyaddhā tvayi śāstraśarīriṇi nityaṃ nibaddhavairāste vayaṃ cānye ca tādṛśāḥ
translation:
We, Daityas, Dānavas, Gandharvas, Siddhas, Vidyādharas, Cāraṇas Yakṣas Ogres (Rākṣasas), goblins (Piśācas), the leaders of evil spirits like Bhūtas and Pramathas and others of our ilk, bear continuous hostility to you who are the receptacle of pure Sattva (unsullied by Rajas and Tamas) and the very embodiment of scriptures (Śāstras).
42(A). Bhāgavata Candrikā’s Text adds:
That some people have attained to the different types of Liberation (from Saṃsāra known as Sālokya (residence in the Deity’s abode), Sāmīpya (vicinity to the Deity), Sārūpya similarity in form to the Deity) and Sātmatā (Coalescence with the Deity) is due to the disposition of their mind, O Lotus-eyed Lord.

devanagari: केचनोद्बद्धवैरेण भक्त्या केचन कामतः । न तथा सत्त्वसंरब्धाः सन्निकृष्टाः सुरादयः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: kecanodbaddhavaireṇa bhaktyā kecana kāmataḥ na tathā sattvasaṃrabdhāḥ sannikṛṣṭāḥ surādayaḥ
translation: Just as by virtue of unflinching devotion to you or by inveterate enmity to you (as in regard to Śiśupāla) or by deep passion of Love for you (as in the case of Gopīs), persons have attained so much close union with you as could not be achieved by gods who possess Sāttvika nature.

devanagari: इदमित्थमिति प्रायस्तव योगेश्वरेश्वर । न विदन्त्यपि योगेशा योगमायां कुतो वयम् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: idamitthamiti prāyastava yogeśvareśvara na vidantyapi yogeśā yogamāyāṃ kuto vayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vid class 2 verb], [nominative plural from √ vid class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ vid class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ vid class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ vid class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ vid class 2 verb]

translation: O Lord of the masters of Yoga! Even Masters of Yoga cannot comprehend the nature and the extent or the inscrutable functions of your Yoga-Māyā. What to speak of Daityas like us?

devanagari: तन्नः प्रसीद निरपेक्षविमृग्ययुष्मत् । पादारविन्दधिषणान्यगृहान्धकूपात् । निष्क्रम्य विश्वशरणाङ्घ्र्युपलब्धवृत्तिः । शान्तो यथैक उत सर्व...
english_devnagari: tannaḥ prasīda nirapekṣavimṛgyayuṣmat pādāravindadhiṣaṇānyagṛhāndhakūpāt niṣkramya viśvaśaraṇāṅghryupalabdhavṛttiḥ śānto yathaika uta sarvasakhaiścarāmi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: Therefore, pray be propitious unto us—Graciously help me to get out of the dark well of house-holder’s life which is different from the Shelter of your lotus-feet which are sought after by persons who are free from desires. Subsisting on whatever (fruit, etc. dropped automatically from the trees) I get at the foot of trees which afford shelter to all, I shall move about alone with a serene mind or (if association with others is necessary) in the company of pious people who are friendly to all.

devanagari: शाध्यस्मानीशितव्येश निष्पापान्कुरु नः प्रभो । पुमान्यच्छ्रद्धयातिष्ठंश्चोदनाया विमुच्यते ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śādhyasmānīśitavyeśa niṣpāpānkuru naḥ prabho pumānyacchraddhayātiṣṭhaṃścodanāyā vimucyate
translation: O Ruler of all jīvas! Be pleased to impart your command to us and absolve us of all our sins. O Lord! For a person who abides by your command faithfully is not subject to the injunctions and interdictions of the scriptures.

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । आसन्मरीचेः षट्पुत्रा ऊर्णायां प्रथमेऽन्तरे । देवाः कं जहसुर्वीक्ष्य सुतं यभितुमुद्यतम् ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca āsanmarīceḥ ṣaṭputrā ūrṇāyāṃ prathame devāḥ kaṃ jahasurvīkṣya sutaṃ yabhitumudyatam

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation:
The glorious Lord said:
“In the first Manvantara, sage Marīci had six sons by his wife Ūrṇā. They were the gods (in that Aeon). They laughed at god Brahmā when they saw that he was ready to cohabit with his daughter.

devanagari: तेनासुरीमगन्योनिमधुनावद्यकर्मणा । हिरण्यकशिपोर्जाता नीतास्ते योगमायया ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: tenāsurīmaganyonimadhunāvadyakarmaṇā hiraṇyakaśiporjātā nītāste yogamāyayā

translation: On account of that ‘misdemeanour’ (as god Brahmā interpreted their laughter and cursed them), they were born in the race of Asuras as the sons of Hiraṇyakaśipu and were taken away by Yogamāyā.

devanagari: देवक्या उदरे जाता राजन्कंसविहिंसिताः । सा तान्शोचत्यात्मजान्स्वांस्त इमेऽध्यासतेऽन्तिके ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: devakyā udare jātā rājankaṃsavihiṃsitāḥ sā tānśocatyātmajānsvāṃsta ime

translation: They were born as sons to Devakī and were (immediately) murdered by Kaṃsa. Even now mother Devakī is lamenting for those children and they are now here with you.

devanagari: इत एतान्प्रणेष्यामो मातृशोकापनुत्तये । ततः शापाद्विनिर्मुक्ता लोकं यास्यन्ति विज्वराः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: ita etānpraṇeṣyāmo mātṛśokāpanuttaye tataḥ śāpādvinirmuktā lokaṃ yāsyanti vijvarāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ yā class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ yā class 2 verb]

translation: For soothing the grief of our mother, we shall take them from this place to her. There they will be liberated from the curse (of Brahmā) and thus freed from the fever of affliction they will return to their celestial region.

devanagari: स्मरोद्गीथः परिष्वङ्गः पतङ्गः क्षुद्रभृद्घृणी । षडिमे मत्प्रसादेन पुनर्यास्यन्ति सद्गतिम् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: smarodgīthaḥ pariṣvaṅgaḥ pataṅgaḥ kṣudrabhṛdghṛṇī ṣaḍime matprasādena punaryāsyanti sadgatim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ yā class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ yā class 2 verb]

translation: Smara, Udgītha, Pariṣvaṅga, Pataṅga, Kṣudrabhṛt and Ghṛṇin—all these six (sons of Marīci) shall attain to their happy state again through my grace.”

devanagari: इत्युक्त्वा तान्समादाय इन्द्रसेनेन पूजितौ । पुनर्द्वारवतीमेत्य मातुः पुत्रानयच्छताम् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: ityuktvā tānsamādāya indrasenena pūjitau punardvāravatīmetya mātuḥ putrānayacchatām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ pūj class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ pūj class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ pūj class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ pūj class 10 verb], [vocative dual from √ pūj class 10 verb], [accusative dual from √ pūj class 10 verb]

translation: Having explained his mission to Bali, he took charge of those sons. Worshipped by Bali, both of them returned to Dvārakā and presented her (dead but now restored) children, to the mother.

devanagari: तान्दृष्ट्वा बालकान्देवी पुत्रस्नेहस्नुतस्तनी । परिष्वज्याङ्कमारोप्य मूर्ध्न्यजिघ्रदभीक्ष्णशः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: tāndṛṣṭvā bālakāndevī putrasnehasnutastanī pariṣvajyāṅkamāropya mūrdhnyajighradabhīkṣṇaśaḥ
translation: Seeking her children (so brought back to her), Devakī had her breasts filled with milk. Hugging them and placing them on her lap, she smelt their crowns repeatedly.

devanagari: अपाययत्स्तनं प्रीता सुतस्पर्शपरिस्नुतम् । मोहिता मायया विष्णोर्यया सृष्टिः प्रवर्तते ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: apāyayatstanaṃ prītā sutasparśaparisnutam mohitā māyayā viṣṇoryayā sṛṣṭiḥ pravartate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ snu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ snu class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ muh ], [vocative plural from √ muh ]
[nominative plural from √ muh ], [vocative plural from √ muh ], [accusative plural from √ muh ]

translation: Overwhelmed with motherly affection at the touch of her children, and being over joyed, she suckled them: she was too much deluded by Viṣṇu’s Māyā by which the whole cycle of creation continues.

devanagari: पीत्वामृतं पयस्तस्याः पीतशेषं गदाभृतः । नारायणाङ्गसंस्पर्श प्रतिलब्धात्मदर्शनाः ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: pītvāmṛtaṃ payastasyāḥ pītaśeṣaṃ gadābhṛtaḥ nārāyaṇāṅgasaṃsparśa pratilabdhātmadarśanāḥ
translation: Having sucked the nectar of her milk which was left over there after Lord Kṛṣṇa’s sucking, and due to the contact of the person of Lord Nārāyaṇa, they regained their self-knowledge.

devanagari: ते नमस्कृत्य गोविन्दं देवकीं पितरं बलम् । मिषतां सर्वभूतानां ययुर्धाम दिवौकसाम् ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: te namaskṛtya govindaṃ devakīṃ pitaraṃ balam miṣatāṃ sarvabhūtānāṃ yayurdhāma divaukasām
translation: Bowing to Kṛṣṇa, Devakī, their father Vasudeva and Balarāma, they ascended through the ethereal region to their places while all beings were witnessing it.

devanagari: तं दृष्ट्वा देवकी देवी मृतागमननिर्गमम् । मेने सुविस्मिता मायां कृष्णस्य रचितां नृप ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ dṛṣṭvā devakī devī mṛtāgamananirgamam mene suvismitā māyāṃ kṛṣṇasya racitāṃ nṛpa

translation: The divine Devakī was so much flabbergasted to see the arrival and departure of her dead children that she thought it to be the illusion created by Kṛṣṇa, O king.

devanagari: एवंविधान्यद्भुतानि कृष्णस्य परमात्मनः । वीर्याण्यनन्तवीर्यस्य सन्त्यनन्तानि भारत ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃvidhānyadbhutāni kṛṣṇasya paramātmanaḥ vīryāṇyanantavīryasya santyanantāni bhārata

translation: O king Parīkṣit! Innumerable are these and such other miraculous exploits of Lord Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme soul of Infinite powers, O descendant of Bharata.

devanagari: श्रीसूत उवाच । य इदमनुशृणोति श्रावयेद्वा मुरारेश् । चरितममृतकीर्तेर्वर्णितं व्यासपुत्रैः । जगदघभिदलं तद्भक्तसत्कर्णपूरं । भगवति कृतचित...
english_devnagari: śrīsūta uvāca ya idamanuśṛṇoti śrāvayedvā murāreś caritamamṛtakīrtervarṇitaṃ vyāsaputraiḥ jagadaghabhidalaṃ tadbhaktasatkarṇapūraṃ bhagavati kṛtacitto yāti tatkṣemadhāma

translation:
S ūta said:
He who devoutly and constantly listens to and recites to others this account of Lord Kṛṣṇa of eternal fame and glory—the account extolled by the venerable son of the sage Vyāsa which absolves the world of all sins, and which fills the ears of Lord’s devotees with delight or is like ornaments to the ears of Lord’s votaries, gets his mind concentrated in the Lord and attains to his region of eternal bliss.
